briefs.title: Dennis Back appointed Chief Election Officer for Burnaby 2026 election
briefs.summary: Council will vote on appointing Dennis Back as Chief Election Officer for the City of Burnaby's 2026 General Local Election, replacing Samantha Pellizzari who is on leave. The election is scheduled for Saturday, October 17, 2026. Back previously served as Burnaby's Municipal Clerk.

key_dates.label: Burnaby 2026 General Local Election date

briefs.source_body:
7.1
APPOINTMENT OF CHIEF ELECTION OFFICER
1.
CEO Appointment 2026-04-14.pdf
Purpose: To recommend the appointment of the Chief Election Officer for the 2026 City of Burnaby General Local Election.

attachments.title: CEO Appointment 2026-04-14.pdf

briefs.title: North Road BIA renewal bylaw heads to council vote — petition fails
briefs.summary: Council will vote on Bylaw No. 14797 to renew the Burnaby North Road Business Improvement Area (BNRBIA) for five years, from April 1, 2026 to March 31, 2031. The 30-day petition period (Feb 25 – Mar 30, 2026) yielded 66 Notices of Opposition from 65 of 120 folio numbers — below the 50% threshold of parcels or assessed value needed to block the renewal. Council may now adopt the bylaw.
briefs.action_required: If you own Class 5 or Class 6 property in the BNRBIA, watch for the council meeting date — the renewal will impose a local service tax levy on your property.

key_dates.label: BNRBIA renewal term begins
BNRBIA renewal term ends

briefs.industries: Retail, Food Service, Landlord & Property Mgmt

briefs.title: Council to vote on BHA 2026 corporate maintenance, appoint directors, auditor KPMG
briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on approving annual corporate maintenance matters for the Burnaby Housing Authority (BHA) for 2026, including appointing KPMG as auditor, selecting December 12, 2026 as the annual reference date, receiving 2025 financial statements, and appointing directors (five independent directors for two-year terms, three city directors and one independent director for three-year terms). These routine shareholder decisions keep the housing corporation in good legal standing.

key_dates.label: BHA annual reference date selected

briefs.title: BHA 2025 audited financials and 2026 budget — $2M operating funding release
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council will consider the Burnaby Housing Authority's (BHA) 2025 audited financial statements and 2026 operating budget. Receipt of these documents satisfies conditions for the City to release $2.00 million in 2026 operating funding under Partnering Agreement No. 1. The BHA reported a clean audit opinion from KPMG, an accumulated surplus of $7.52 million, and cash holdings of $31.75 million as of December 31, 2025.

briefs.title: Burnaby e-bike share pilot: parking zones, speed rules, operator fees
briefs.summary: Council will consider amendments to three bylaws to launch an e-bike share pilot and address the provincial e-scooter pilot. Key changes: shared e-bikes and e-scooters must park only in designated Shared Micromobility Parking Zones; operators need a City agreement and will pay fees based on device count and usage. E-scooters are banned on major road networks and arterial roads except in protected cycle lanes or multi-use pathways. Riding on sidewalks is prohibited unless a traffic control device permits it.
briefs.action_required: Watch for council decision on April 14, 2026 — if approved, businesses near proposed parking zones may see changes in sidewalk and street use.

key_dates.label: Burnaby council decision on e-bike/e-scooter bylaw amendments

briefs.source_body:

briefs.title: Burnaby must submit housing target progress report to BC by April 14, 2026
briefs.summary: Council will consider authorizing submission of Burnaby's Housing Target Progress Report to the BC Ministry of Housing and Municipal Affairs, as required by the Housing Supply Act. The report covers the first six-month reporting period (Sept 1, 2025 – Feb 28, 2026) and shows 910 net new housing units completed, against a Year 1 target of 1,536. The report must be received by Council resolution within 45 days of the reporting period end (by April 14, 2026) and submitted to the Province as soon as practicable after that.
briefs.action_required: No action required from business owners; this is a municipal compliance report.

key_dates.label: Burnaby housing target progress report submission deadline

briefs.title: Burnaby to join Building Benchmark BC Year 7 energy program
briefs.summary: Council will consider authorizing the City of Burnaby to participate in Year 7 of the Building Benchmark BC (BBBC) program, a voluntary provincial program that tracks and reports energy use in large buildings. If approved, the city would share building energy data with the province to help identify ways to save energy and reduce emissions. The decision is not final — council will vote on this proposal at an upcoming meeting.
briefs.action_required: No action needed from you now. Watch for council's decision — if approved, building owners may need to prepare energy data for future reporting.

briefs.title: FortisBC natural gas right-of-way on Burnaby Mountain — Council approval April 14, 2026
briefs.summary: Council will consider granting a Statutory Right-of-Way (a legal permission to use specific land for a specific purpose) to FortisBC Energy Inc. over portions of 7950 Pandora Street and 1200 Gaglardi Way. This would allow FortisBC to install, operate, and maintain natural gas infrastructure to service the Burnaby Mountain Conservation Area, including the Mintara restaurant. The City is not requesting compensation for the right-of-way. The decision is up for approval at the April 14, 2026 council meeting.

key_dates.label: Council decision on FortisBC right-of-way — Burnaby

briefs.title: Council to vote on $1.35M modular four-plex pilot at 8315 14th Avenue
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council is set to vote on awarding a $1,354,463 contract to Echelon Creative Inc. to build a modular four-plex housing unit at 8315 14th Avenue as a pilot project. The three-storey, 5,319 sq ft building will include four residential units (one accessible) with private outdoor space. The project is supported by $150,000 from Fortis BC and $200,000 from the National Research Council. Construction is expected to take 6–8 months, with a useful life of 50–60 years (up to 80 with maintenance). The City will retain ownership and lease the units.
briefs.action_required: Watch for the council decision on April 14, 2026 — if approved, this pilot could affect future modular housing development opportunities in Burnaby.

key_dates.label: Council vote on modular housing four-plex contract award

briefs.title: Council to vote on $2.4M Jerry Rogers Creek culvert replacement contract
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council will vote on awarding a $2,404,076 contract to HANNA Infrastructure Ltd. for the Jerry Rogers Creek Culvert Replacement Project. The project includes installing 45 lineal metres of 1.8m x 1.8m box culvert, 40 lineal metres of DI watermain, storm pipe tie-ins, and replacement of a Metro Vancouver sanitary main. The new culvert is designed to meet 100-year climate change flows.

briefs.title: Council to vote on $1.36M contract for Central Valley Greenway culvert and paving work
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council will vote on awarding a $1,360,391 contract to Drake Excavating (2016) Ltd. for the Central Valley Greenway Culvert Replacement and Paving Project. The work includes replacing 16 culverts between Sperling Avenue and Douglas Road, with major replacements at Beecher Creek (1100mm twin) and Crab Apple Creek (1200, 1500mm twin), plus a Beaver Creek culvert at Deer Lake, and final paving of the trail.

briefs.title: Council to vote on $450,000 contract change for cellular voice and data services
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council is set to vote on approving a $450,000 contract change (including taxes) to Rogers Communications Canada Inc. for cellular voice and data services. The change covers a month-to-month extension for up to 9 months while the city transitions to a new provider selected through RFP #18-02-20. The total estimated contract value including this change is $3,495,900. The decision does not directly affect private businesses or residents.

briefs.title: Council to vote on $579,600 HVAC contract change for city buildings
briefs.summary: Council will vote on approving a $579,600 contract change (amendment) to Just Mechanical Ltd. for HVAC maintenance and repair at various City-owned buildings. The change covers interim services while the City seeks new competitive bids. The total estimated contract value including this change is $5,412,750. Final payment will be based on actual services delivered at the hourly rate submitted.
briefs.action_required: No action required from business owners; this is a City internal contract decision.

key_dates.label: Council vote on HVAC contract change

briefs.title: Burnaby Council to vote on $394,942 Cisco WebEx licence renewal for 2026–2029
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council will vote on a three-year contract renewal with Long View Systems Corporation for Cisco WebEx Contact Center licenses at an estimated cost of $394,942 including taxes. The renewal covers May 5, 2026 to May 4, 2029. The original contract was awarded in December 2024 for $373,423. The total estimated contract value including prior renewals is $816,202.

briefs.title: Council to vote on $1.67M fire protection contract renewal with A.W Fireguard
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council will vote on a three-year contract renewal with A.W Fireguard Supplies (1991) Ltd. for fire protection system installation, maintenance, and repair services, valued at $1,666,061 including taxes. The renewal would run from July 1, 2026 to June 30, 2029, with final payment based on actual services delivered at hourly rates. The original contract included optional renewal years, so no new bidding is required.

briefs.title: Burnaby Lake Overpass plaque wording approved for April 26 opening
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council approved the wording and design for a commemorative plaque at the Burnaby Lake Overpass. The plaque will be installed as part of the grand opening on April 26, 2026, which includes a public event. The overpass connects Glencarin Drive to Claude Avenue/McCarthy Court, providing a pedestrian-cyclist crossing over Highway 1 linking Burnaby Lake and Deer Lake.

key_dates.label: Burnaby Lake Overpass grand opening and public event

briefs.title: Burnaby council to vote on external financial review to curb tax hikes
briefs.summary: Council will vote on hiring an external reviewer to assess the city's financial planning, operating practices, and capital investment priorities. The review aims to mitigate risks of significant tax increases and rising service costs for businesses and residents, driven by a sharp decline in density bonus revenues and higher procurement costs.
briefs.action_required: Watch for the council decision — if approved, this could affect future tax and service costs for your business.
briefs.source_body:

1.
FMC - External Review of City Financial Practices 2026-04-14.pdf
Purpose: To seek Council approval to engage an external reviewer to help safeguard the City's assets and financial standing, and to mitigate the risk of significant tax increases and rising service costs for businesses and residents.

briefs.title: Proposed: 20-storey, 215-unit rental rezoning at 5035 Beresford Street
briefs.summary: Burnaby Council is set to vote on forwarding rezoning application REZ #25-11 for a 20-storey apartment building with 215 market rental units (32 studio, 98 one-bedroom, 65 two-bedroom, 20 three-bedroom) at 5035 Beresford Street to First and Second Reading. The site is currently zoned M4 Special Industrial and would change to CD Comprehensive Development District. The project includes 38 residential vehicle parking spaces (100% EV ready), 12 visitor spaces, 441 secured bicycle spaces, and 47 visitor bicycle spaces. Prerequisites include a Housing Agreement for non-market rental units under the Inclusionary Rental Requirements Bylaw, a 1.9m road dedication along Beresford Street, and connection to the City's District Energy Utility system when available.
briefs.action_required: Watch for the public hearing date — if you live or own a business nearby, you may want to attend and share your views.

key_dates.label: Council vote to forward rezoning to First and Second Reading

briefs.title: Mayor's Task Force on Burnaby Economic Growth — recommendations due June 23, 2026
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council received an interim update on the Mayor's Task Force on Burnaby Economic Growth and Resilience. The Task Force has identified strategic priorities and is validating them through a business community survey and 10 stakeholder interviews conducted March 13–April 2, 2026. The Task Force will reconvene April 16, 2026 for three additional sessions through end of May to finalize actionable recommendations, which will be presented to Council on June 23, 2026. No decisions or new rules have been proposed yet.

key_dates.label: Task Force reconvenes to finalize recommendations
Task Force recommendations presented to Council

briefs.source_body:

briefs.title: Developer forfeits $1,363,500 security for public pier at 8351 Fraser Reach Court
briefs.summary: Council will consider what to do with a $1,363,500 security deposit that developer Oxford Properties has forfeited instead of building a promised public amenity pier at 8351 Fraser Reach Court. The developer chose to give up the money rather than construct the pier, which was part of the original rezoning approval for the Riverbend Business Park. The City now holds the forfeited funds and is waiting for Council to decide how to use them, with the covenant allowing the funds to go toward the pier or an alternative public amenity along the Fraser Foreshore or elsewhere in Burnaby.
briefs.source_body:
9.2
AMENITY PIER COVENANT - DEVELOPER ELECTION TO FORFEIT SECURITY
1.
Amenity Pier Covenant  Developer Election to Forfeit Security 2026-04-14.pdf
2.
Attachment 1 - Sketch 1 and 2 (Amenity Pier Covenant) 2026-04-14.pdf
Purpose: To provide Council with information regarding the owner’s election under a Section 219 Covenant registered on title at 8351 Fraser Reach Court to forfeit the posted security and not proceed with construction of the public amenity pier contemplated under the Riverbend Business Park rezoning approvals, and the City’s holding of the forfeited security pending future Council direction.

briefs.title: Burnaby North Road BIA bylaw: $1.76M levy over 5 years faces opposition
briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on final adoption of the Burnaby North Road Business Improvement Area (BIA) Bylaw 2026. The bylaw would impose a local service tax on Class 5 (light industry) and Class 6 (business/other) properties based on assessed value, raising up to $1,755,147.81 over a 5-year term (April 1, 2026 – March 31, 2031). Annual grants to the association range from $317,954.94 (Year 1) to $385,740.60 (Year 5). Enough business owners have signed petitions against it, so the bylaw may be rejected or changed.
briefs.action_required: If you own a Class 5 or Class 6 property on Burnaby North Road, watch for the council decision — this could affect your property taxes and local fees.

key_dates.label: Burnaby North Road BIA bylaw final adoption vote
BIA term begins — levy takes effect
BIA term ends
